pages.text: One sunny morning, <name> planted a tiny seed in the garden. He dug a small hole, placed the seed carefully, and covered it with soft soil. “Grow well, little seed!” he whispered.
Days passed, but nothing happened. <name> scratched his head. “Why isn’t it growing?” he wondered.
Suddenly, a talking flower popped up nearby. “Hello, <name>! Seeds need more than soil. They need water, sunlight, and love,” it said, swaying in the breeze.
A friendly worm wriggled out from the dirt. “And patience!” he added. “I help make the soil cozy for your seed.”
A warm sunbeam shone down, tickling <name>’s nose. “Water me gently, little gardener,” it seemed to say.
<name> followed their advice. Every day he gave the seed a little water, made sure the sun could reach it, and whispered encouraging words. He watched the soil carefully, waiting and hoping.
One morning, <name> noticed a tiny green sprout peeking through the dirt. “It’s growing!” he cheered. The talking flower clapped its petals, and the worm danced happily in the soil.

“See, <name>, seeds grow when you give them water, sunlight, and patience. That’s how plants become big and strong!” <name> smiled, feeling proud. He had planted a tiny seed and helped it grow, learning that care and patience make wonderful things happen. Happily ever after.
